justification: Safe. Source: Form input value; localStorage. Sink: safe:input.value assignment (text field). The data is assigned to a non-HTML sink (event handler function, input.value, or similar), so it is never parsed as markup.

notes: Parsed JSON from storage via textContent - safe
justification: Safe. Source: localStorage. Sink: safe:textContent. Tainted data is written via a non-parsing sink, so any markup is rendered as inert text or stored in a non-executing attribute.

notes: postMessage data straight to innerHTML with no origin check - vulnerable
justification: OWASP DOM XSS. Source: postMessage event.data. Sink: DANGEROUS:innerHTML. Mitigation: none. Tainted data reaches an HTML-parsing sink without adequate encoding or allowlisting; an attacker controlling the source can inject script-executing markup (e.g. ).

notes: Message body replaced from message data - no origin check
justification: OWASP DOM XSS. Source: postMessage event.data. Sink: DANGEROUS:innerHTML. Mitigation: none. Tainted data reaches an HTML-parsing sink without adequate encoding or allowlisting; an attacker controlling the source can inject script-executing markup (e.g. ).

OWASP DOM XSS (script-execution sink). Source: postMessage event.data. Sink: DANGEROUS:eval. Mitigation: none. Untrusted data is parsed and executed as JavaScript, giving immediate code execution in the page origin.
Safe. Source: postMessage event.data. Sink: safe:textContent. Mitigation: postMessage origin check. Untrusted data is constrained before reaching the sink, breaking the injection path.

Safe. Source: postMessage event.data. Sink: DANGEROUS:innerHTML. Mitigation: DOMPurify sanitisation; postMessage origin check. Untrusted data is constrained before reaching the sink, breaking the injection path.
